  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:249:delegated → completed | failed  (on task-terminal 
event from final chain link)
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:250:{pending | delegated | paused_*} → cancelled  (on 
user cancel)
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:332:2. **Drain queued chain-continuation jobs** — remove 
FIFO-queued continuation entries (§ 3.17 item 5) for this task from the scheduler queue. Atomic with step 3.
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:333:3. **Prevent further chain dispatch** — set 
`agent_runs.status = 'cancelled'` via the optimistic predicate `UPDATE agent_runs SET status='cancelled' WHERE id = $1 
AND status IN 
('delegated','paused_for_chain_continuation','paused_chain_failure','paused_budget_exceeded','pending')`. 0 
rows-affected means the task is already in a terminal state; return `409` with the current state.
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:334:4. **Emit the task-terminal event** — 
`operator-session.task_cancelled` (§ 4.7). The finaliser performs full rollup.
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:335:5. **Schedule profile retention/GC** — set 
`operator_task_profiles.scheduled_gc_at = now() + INTERVAL '48 hours'` per § 3.15 item 4 (default retention).
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:434:4. **Chain-link dispatch.** End of chain link N 
writes `checkpoint_payload` and emits `'operator-session-completed'` (the standard adapter terminal event, with status 
`'completed'` and a non-null `checkpoint_payload` field — distinct from the task-terminal `'completed'`). The 
finaliser handler distinguishes (branches evaluated in order; first match wins):
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:435:   - `completed` with non-null `checkpoint_payload` 
AND consumed-budget-minutes ≥ pinned `settings_snapshot.per_task_budget_cap_minutes` → task transitions to 
`'paused_budget_exceeded'`; do NOT enqueue the next chain link.
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:436:   - `completed` with non-null `checkpoint_payload` 
AND (`now() - first_operator_run.started_at`) ≥ pinned `settings_snapshot.max_wall_clock_per_task_days` → task 
transitions to `'paused_budget_exceeded'` with `failure_reason='max_wall_clock_exceeded'`; do NOT enqueue the next 
chain link.
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:437:   - `completed` with non-null `checkpoint_payload` 
AND the parent task is at max chain length (`chain_seq >= settings_snapshot.max_chain_length`) → task-terminal 
`'failed'` with `failure_reason='max_chain_length_reached'` (rolls up to `agent_runs.status='failed'`).
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:438:   - `completed` with non-null `checkpoint_payload` 
(none of the above caps tripped) → dispatch next chain link via the `'operator-session-dispatch-next-chain-link'` 
queue.
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:534:- `max_chain_length` — finaliser decision table, per 
§ 3.14 item 4 (`chain_seq >= cap` branch).
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:535:- `max_wall_clock_per_task_days` — finaliser decision 
table, per § 3.14 item 4 (`now() - first_operator_run.started_at >= cap` → `paused_budget_exceeded` with 
`failure_reason='max_wall_clock_exceeded'`).
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:536:- `per_task_budget_cap_minutes` — finaliser decision 
table, per § 3.14 item 4 (consumed budget branch) and § 3.17 item 4.
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:537:- `concurrent_operator_sessions_cap` — adapter 
`dispatch()`, per § 3.17 item 5 (advisory-lock pattern).
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:567:
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:568:1. **Backoff retry on transient failure.** Chain-link 
dispatch is retried with exponential backoff: 1 min, 5 min, 15 min. After **3 consecutive failures** 
(`agent_runs.operator_chain_failure_count >= 3`), the task transitions to `paused_chain_failure`.
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:569:
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:570:   The retry counter 
(`agent_runs.operator_chain_failure_count`, per § 3.4) resets on:
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:571:   - A user-initiated retry (via the per-task "Retry" 
action).
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:572:   - A successful chain-link dispatch (i.e. counter 
is "consecutive failures since last successful dispatch", not lifetime).
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:605:3. **User notification.** On `paused_chain_failure` 
transition, notify the assigned user via the existing inbox/notification path. Notification payload includes the 
failure class and the suggested action (Retry / Cancel). User actions:
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:606:   - **Retry** — resets retry counter, dispatches 
next chain link from the current checkpoint (no fresh profile). Audit event `task.operator.chain_failure_retried`.
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:607:   - **Cancel** — § 3.10 cancellation path.
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:608:
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:609:4. **Budget-cap auto-pause and accounting.** Hitting 
the per-task budget cap is NOT a failure — it is a deliberate auto-pause. Task transitions to `paused_budget_exceeded`.
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:610:
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:611:   **Accounting rules:**
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:614:   - When consumption hits the per-task budget cap 
mid-chain-link, the running chain link MUST checkpoint and pause at the **next resumable boundary** (NOT hard-kill), 
UNLESS the chain link also breaches the hard safety limit (soft cap + grace), in which case it terminates per § 3.14 
item 3 hard-cap behaviour.
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:615:   - Budget extension actions are additive (`new_cap 
= old_cap + extension_minutes`), never absolute resets, and write `task.operator_budget.extended` (§ 4.9) with 
`actor_user_id`, `extension_minutes`, `request_id`, `source`.
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:616:   - The user can extend at increments of 60 minutes 
(UI offers presets +1000, +3000, plus a custom-amount input bounded 60..60000 with 60-min step per mockup 
`r16-modal-budget-exceeded-autopause.html`).

  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1179:1. Reads the parent `agent_runs` row.
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1180:2. Proceed only when `agent_runs.status` is one of 
`'pending'`, `'delegated'`, `'paused_for_chain_continuation'`, `'paused_chain_failure'` (after user-initiated retry — 
counter has been reset), or `'paused_budget_exceeded'` (after user-initiated extension). Each paused-state entry MUST 
carry a queued-job "reason" tag matching the resume action (`'continuation'`, `'retry'`, `'budget_extension'`); the 
handler asserts the tag matches the current task state before dispatching. Otherwise no-op (idempotent re-delivery).
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1181:3. Calls 
`executionBackendRegistry.get('operator_managed').dispatch({ agentRunId, ... })` — re-uses § 7.1 inline path.
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1182:4. On success: writes 
`agent_runs.status='delegated'` via the optimistic predicate `UPDATE agent_runs SET status='delegated', 
operator_chain_failure_count=0 WHERE id=$1 AND status IN 
('pending','delegated','paused_for_chain_continuation','paused_chain_failure','paused_budget_exceeded')`. 0 
rows-affected means the task is in a terminal state — no-op.
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1183:5. On failure with `'transient'` failure class: 
increments `agent_runs.operator_chain_failure_count` (per § 3.4 writer rules — predicate `UPDATE ... WHERE status IN 
('delegated','paused_for_chain_continuation')`); enqueues a delayed re-dispatch (1 min → 5 min → 15 min via pg-boss's 
`startAfter` parameter).
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1184:6. On the 3rd consecutive failure: writes 
`agent_runs.status='paused_chain_failure'`, emits `'operator-session.task_paused_chain_failure'` event, sends user 
notification.
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1185:7. On failure with `'permanent' | 'auth' | 
'profile_corruption'` failure class: bypasses the backoff and goes directly to `paused_chain_failure`.
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1250:| `operator_runs` insert (chain-link start) | 
**state-based** | optimistic predicate: `INSERT ... WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM operator_runs WHERE 
agent_run_id=$1 AND attempt_number=$2 AND chain_seq=$3)`. 0 rows-affected = a concurrent dispatcher already started 
this chain link. UNIQUE `(agent_run_id, attempt_number, chain_seq)` per § 3.3 is the DB-level backstop. |
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1251:| `operator_runs` terminal UPDATE | **state-based** 
| optimistic predicate: `UPDATE ... WHERE status IN ('pending','running')`. 0 rows-affected = already terminal. |
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1252:| `agent_runs` terminal UPDATE (task-terminal) | 
**state-based** | optimistic predicate: `UPDATE ... WHERE status IN ('delegated','paused_*')` (the closed set of 
pre-terminal states). 0 rows-affected = already terminal; finaliser logs and returns 200 idempotent-hit. |
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1253:| `agent_runs` pause UPDATE 
(`paused_for_chain_continuation`) | **state-based** | optimistic predicate: `UPDATE ... WHERE status='delegated'`. 0 
rows-affected = a concurrent path already paused or terminated. |
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1254:| `operator_task_profiles` insert (first chain link) 
| **key-based** | unique constraint: `(task_id, attempt_number)`. 23505 → catch and return the existing row 
(idempotent-hit). |
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1285:| Cron GC + admin debug-retention | the GC scan 
reads `scheduled_gc_at`; if an admin extends retention between scan and update, the row's `scheduled_gc_at` moves 
forward AND the cron's UPDATE-WHERE predicate (`status='scheduled_gc' AND scheduled_gc_at <= now()`) returns 0 rows on 
the second pass. Safe. |
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1286:| Fresh-profile-restart racing in-flight chain link 
| the restart route requires the task to be in a paused state. Optimistic predicate: `UPDATE agent_runs SET ... WHERE 
status IN ('paused_chain_failure','paused_budget_exceeded','paused_for_chain_continuation')`. Terminal `failed | 
completed | cancelled` is never restarted under the same `agent_run_id` (§ 10.7 forbidden transitions). A 
`running`/`delegated` chain link blocks restart. |

  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1288:### 10.4 Terminal event guarantees
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1306:- A chain link that started but didn't finish a task 
→ `'chain_link_completed'` with checkpoint (NOT silent partial).
> docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1307:- A task that hit the budget cap mid-chain-link → 
the chain link `'chain_link_completed'` with checkpoint; the task `'task_paused_budget_exceeded'` (NOT a silent 
partial on the task).
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1308:- A task that exhausted max chain length → 
`'task_failed'` with `failure_reason='max_chain_length_reached'` (NOT silent).
  docs\superpowers\specs\2026-05-12-operator-backend-spec.md:1309:- A fallback-engaged chain link → mixed cost rows 
per § 3.12; both row types reflect the boundary explicitly.

codex
1. Section reference: § 3.4, § 3.10 step 3, § 10.7  
   Description: The spec both allows `pending → cancelled` and forbids `pending → cancelled`, so cancellation predicates are impossible to implement consistently.  
   Suggested fix: Change § 10.7 forbidden list to: “Forbidden: `pending → completed | failed` without going through `delegated`; `pending → cancelled` is allowed only for user cancellation before first dispatch.”  
   Severity: critical

2. Section reference: § 10.1 `agent_runs` terminal UPDATE, § 10.7  
   Description: The generic predicate `WHERE status IN ('delegated','paused_*')` permits paused states to transition directly to `completed` or `failed`, violating the state machine.  
   Suggested fix: Replace with: “Task-terminal `completed`/`failed` writers MUST use `WHERE status='delegated'`; task-terminal `cancelled` MUST use `WHERE status IN ('pending','delegated','paused_for_chain_continuation','paused_chain_failure','paused_budget_exceeded')`.”  
   Severity: critical

3. Section reference: § 3.4 writer rules, § 7.3 steps 2 and 5  
   Description: Retry dispatch can start from `paused_chain_failure` or `paused_budget_exceeded`, but failure-counter increments only match `delegated` and `paused_for_chain_continuation`, so retries from those states can fail without counting toward pause semantics.  
   Suggested fix: Change the increment predicate to: “`WHERE id=$1 AND status IN ('delegated','paused_for_chain_continuation','paused_chain_failure','paused_budget_exceeded')` when the queued-job reason matches the current paused state.”  
   Severity: important

4. Section reference: § 7.4, § 10.4  
   Description: Queued progress events can be delivered after a chain-link terminal event, violating the “no further events after terminal” guarantee.  
   Suggested fix: Add: “`operatorSessionProgressedHandler` MUST update and emit only when `operator_runs.status='running'`; if the row is terminal, the handler drops the event without WebSocket or Run Trace emission.”  
   Severity: critical

5. Section reference: § 7.4  
   Description: `greatest(last_progress_at, $event_timestamp)` is not safe for the first progress event because PostgreSQL returns NULL when one argument is NULL.  
   Suggested fix: Replace the SQL with: “`last_progress_at = greatest(coalesce(last_progress_at, '-infinity'::timestamptz), $event_timestamp), step_count = greatest(step_count, $step_index)`.”  
   Severity: important

6. Section reference: § 3.16, § 3.3, § 4.11  
   Description: The spec says concurrency-cap enforcement reads `operator_runs.settings_snapshot` for in-flight work, but § 7.1 dispatch reads live settings for each dispatch; chain-continuation dispatches are both “in-flight task” and “new dispatch.”  
   Suggested fix: Add: “`concurrent_operator_sessions_cap` is always read from the live `subaccount_operator_settings` row at dispatch time; it is included in `settings_snapshot` for audit/incident context only and is not an in-flight enforcement source.”  
   Severity: important

7. Section reference: § 3.4, § 3.14 item 4, § 3.16 enforcement points  
   Description: `paused_budget_exceeded` is defined as budget-cap reached, but max wall-clock expiry also transitions to `paused_budget_exceeded` with `failure_reason='max_wall_clock_exceeded'`.  
   Suggested fix: Change the state definition to: “`paused_budget_exceeded` — task paused because a time-budget guard fired, including per-task budget cap or max wall-clock per task; `failure_reason` distinguishes `budget_cap_exceeded` from `max_wall_clock_exceeded`.”  
   Severity: important

8. Section reference: § 3.17 item 4, § 4.9  
   Description: The budget-extension audit event is named two different ways: `task.operator_budget.extended` and `task.operator.budget_extended`.  
   Suggested fix: Pick one name and update both sections, e.g. “Budget extension actions write audit event `task.operator.budget_extended` with `agent_run_id, actor_user_id, extension_minutes, request_id, source`.”  
   Severity: important

9. Section reference: § 3.7 item 2  
   Description: `ApiKeyEnvelope` is introduced in the fallback return type but no concrete contract, file path, or fields are defined.  
   Suggested fix: Add: “`ApiKeyEnvelope` is declared in `server/services/credentialBrokerService.ts` with shape `{ credentialId, connectionId, authType: 'api_key', provider, issuedAt, expiresAt: null }`; raw API key material remains broker-internal and never leaves the broker.”  
   Severity: important

10. Section reference: § 3.3, § 6.1-6.3, § 5.2  
    Description: The spec says RLS manifest entries are added “in the same migration,” but `server/config/rlsProtectedTables.ts` is TypeScript and cannot be modified by SQL migrations.  
    Suggested fix: Replace “in the same migration” with: “in the same implementation chunk/commit as the table migration, with `policyMigration` pointing at the SQL migration.”  
    Severity: minor

11. Section reference: § 5.3, § 14 chunks 2 and 15  
    Description: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` is modified in the chunk plan but is missing from the § 5 file inventory lock.  
    Suggested fix: Add to § 5.3: “`.github/workflows/ci.yml` — wire `verify-execution-capability-references.sh`, `verify-operator-event-registry.sh`, and `verify-no-checkpoint-logging.sh` into CI.”  
    Severity: important

12. Section reference: § 14 chunk 8, § 5  
    Description: “Permission registry entry” and “Route registration in the main routes index” are not concrete file paths, and the likely permission/route files are absent from the inventory.  
    Suggested fix: Replace with concrete paths, e.g. “`server/lib/permissions.ts` and `server/services/permissionSeedService.ts` add `SUBACCOUNT_OPERATOR_SETTINGS_WRITE`; `server/index.ts` mounts `operatorSessions`, `subaccountOperatorSettings`, and `operatorTasks` routes.”  
    Severity: important

13. Section reference: § 13.1, § 5.3, § 14 chunks 10-13  
    Description: § 5.3 and § 13.1 require changes to `OpenTaskView.tsx`, `ChatPane.tsx`, `ActivityPane.tsx`, and `FilesTab.tsx`, but no implementation chunk owns those edits.  
    Suggested fix: Add those files to Chunk 11 with: “Wire operator terminal/fallback/cost/artefact UI states in `OpenTaskView.tsx`, `ChatPane.tsx`, `ActivityPane.tsx`, and `FilesTab.tsx`.”  
    Severity: important

14. Section reference: § 10.6  
    Description: `OperatorBackendConflictError` and its mapper middleware are introduced without a concrete file path or ownership in § 5 / § 14.  
    Suggested fix: Add to § 5.1 or § 5.3: “`server/services/operatorBackendErrors.ts` exports `OperatorBackendConflictError`; existing error-mapper file `<exact path>` maps it to the 4xx responses listed in § 10.6.”  
    Severity: important

15. Section reference: § 3.2, § 4.1, § 5.3  
    Description: The spec frames only `long_running` as the new capability, but § 4.1 and § 5.3 also add `session_identity`; implementers cannot tell whether `session_identity` is already shipped by Spec C or part of this change.  
    Suggested fix: Change § 3.2 to: “This spec adds both `'long_running'` and `'session_identity'` to `ExecutionCapability` if not already present; `session_identity` is declared on `operator_managed` but is non-gating at runtime in V1.”  
    Severity: important

   Severity: critical

3. Section reference: § 3.4 writer rules, § 7.3 steps 2 and 5  
   Description: Retry dispatch can start from `paused_chain_failure` or `paused_budget_exceeded`, but failure-counter increments only match `delegated` and `paused_for_chain_continuation`, so retries from those states can fail without counting toward pause semantics.  
   Suggested fix: Change the increment predicate to: “`WHERE id=$1 AND status IN ('delegated','paused_for_chain_continuation','paused_chain_failure','paused_budget_exceeded')` when the queued-job reason matches the current paused state.”  
   Severity: important

4. Section reference: § 7.4, § 10.4  
   Description: Queued progress events can be delivered after a chain-link terminal event, violating the “no further events after terminal” guarantee.  
   Suggested fix: Add: “`operatorSessionProgressedHandler` MUST update and emit only when `operator_runs.status='running'`; if the row is terminal, the handler drops the event without WebSocket or Run Trace emission.”  
   Severity: critical

5. Section reference: § 7.4  
   Description: `greatest(last_progress_at, $event_timestamp)` is not safe for the first progress event because PostgreSQL returns NULL when one argument is NULL.  
   Suggested fix: Replace the SQL with: “`last_progress_at = greatest(coalesce(last_progress_at, '-infinity'::timestamptz), $event_timestamp), step_count = greatest(step_count, $step_index)`.”  
   Severity: important

6. Section reference: § 3.16, § 3.3, § 4.11  
   Description: The spec says concurrency-cap enforcement reads `operator_runs.settings_snapshot` for in-flight work, but § 7.1 dispatch reads live settings for each dispatch; chain-continuation dispatches are both “in-flight task” and “new dispatch.”  
   Suggested fix: Add: “`concurrent_operator_sessions_cap` is always read from the live `subaccount_operator_settings` row at dispatch time; it is included in `settings_snapshot` for audit/incident context only and is not an in-flight enforcement source.”  
   Severity: important

7. Section reference: § 3.4, § 3.14 item 4, § 3.16 enforcement points  
   Description: `paused_budget_exceeded` is defined as budget-cap reached, but max wall-clock expiry also transitions to `paused_budget_exceeded` with `failure_reason='max_wall_clock_exceeded'`.  
   Suggested fix: Change the state definition to: “`paused_budget_exceeded` — task paused because a time-budget guard fired, including per-task budget cap or max wall-clock per task; `failure_reason` distinguishes `budget_cap_exceeded` from `max_wall_clock_exceeded`.”  
   Severity: important

8. Section reference: § 3.17 item 4, § 4.9  
   Description: The budget-extension audit event is named two different ways: `task.operator_budget.extended` and `task.operator.budget_extended`.  
   Suggested fix: Pick one name and update both sections, e.g. “Budget extension actions write audit event `task.operator.budget_extended` with `agent_run_id, actor_user_id, extension_minutes, request_id, source`.”  
   Severity: important

9. Section reference: § 3.7 item 2  
   Description: `ApiKeyEnvelope` is introduced in the fallback return type but no concrete contract, file path, or fields are defined.  
   Suggested fix: Add: “`ApiKeyEnvelope` is declared in `server/services/credentialBrokerService.ts` with shape `{ credentialId, connectionId, authType: 'api_key', provider, issuedAt, expiresAt: null }`; raw API key material remains broker-internal and never leaves the broker.”  
   Severity: important

10. Section reference: § 3.3, § 6.1-6.3, § 5.2  
    Description: The spec says RLS manifest entries are added “in the same migration,” but `server/config/rlsProtectedTables.ts` is TypeScript and cannot be modified by SQL migrations.  
    Suggested fix: Replace “in the same migration” with: “in the same implementation chunk/commit as the table migration, with `policyMigration` pointing at the SQL migration.”  
    Severity: minor

11. Section reference: § 5.3, § 14 chunks 2 and 15  
    Description: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` is modified in the chunk plan but is missing from the § 5 file inventory lock.  
    Suggested fix: Add to § 5.3: “`.github/workflows/ci.yml` — wire `verify-execution-capability-references.sh`, `verify-operator-event-registry.sh`, and `verify-no-checkpoint-logging.sh` into CI.”  
    Severity: important

12. Section reference: § 14 chunk 8, § 5  
    Description: “Permission registry entry” and “Route registration in the main routes index” are not concrete file paths, and the likely permission/route files are absent from the inventory.  
    Suggested fix: Replace with concrete paths, e.g. “`server/lib/permissions.ts` and `server/services/permissionSeedService.ts` add `SUBACCOUNT_OPERATOR_SETTINGS_WRITE`; `server/index.ts` mounts `operatorSessions`, `subaccountOperatorSettings`, and `operatorTasks` routes.”  
    Severity: important

13. Section reference: § 13.1, § 5.3, § 14 chunks 10-13  
    Description: § 5.3 and § 13.1 require changes to `OpenTaskView.tsx`, `ChatPane.tsx`, `ActivityPane.tsx`, and `FilesTab.tsx`, but no implementation chunk owns those edits.  
    Suggested fix: Add those files to Chunk 11 with: “Wire operator terminal/fallback/cost/artefact UI states in `OpenTaskView.tsx`, `ChatPane.tsx`, `ActivityPane.tsx`, and `FilesTab.tsx`.”  
    Severity: important

14. Section reference: § 10.6  
    Description: `OperatorBackendConflictError` and its mapper middleware are introduced without a concrete file path or ownership in § 5 / § 14.  
    Suggested fix: Add to § 5.1 or § 5.3: “`server/services/operatorBackendErrors.ts` exports `OperatorBackendConflictError`; existing error-mapper file `<exact path>` maps it to the 4xx responses listed in § 10.6.”  
    Severity: important

15. Section reference: § 3.2, § 4.1, § 5.3  
    Description: The spec frames only `long_running` as the new capability, but § 4.1 and § 5.3 also add `session_identity`; implementers cannot tell whether `session_identity` is already shipped by Spec C or part of this change.  
    Suggested fix: Change § 3.2 to: “This spec adds both `'long_running'` and `'session_identity'` to `ExecutionCapability` if not already present; `session_identity` is declared on `operator_managed` but is non-gating at runtime in V1.”  
    Severity: important
